name: Morphology of Flowering Plants
full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Morphology of Flowering Plants

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Morphology of Flowering Plants

questions.question: Monstera has:
questions.question_raw: Monstera has:
questions.explanation: In some plants, like grass, Monstera, and the banyan tree, roots arise from parts of the plant other than the radicle and are called adventitious roots.

questions.question: The root is covered at the apex by a thimble-like structure called:
questions.question_raw: The root is covered at the apex by a thimble-like structure called:
questions.explanation: The root is covered at the apex by a thimble-like structure called the root cap.

questions.question: The part that develops from the plumule of the embryo of a germinating seed is:
questions.question_raw: The part that develops from the plumule of the embryo of a germinating seed is:
questions.explanation: The stem develops from the plumule of the embryo of a germinating seed.

questions.question: Rhizophora possess________.
questions.question_raw: Rhizophora possess________.
questions.explanation: Rhizophora possess Pneumatophores

questions.question: Roots developed from parts of the plant other than radicle are called
questions.question_raw: Roots developed from parts of the plant other than radicle are called
questions.explanation: Roots developed from parts of the plant other than radicle are called adventitious roots.

questions.question: The small lateral outgrowth of the leaf base which protects the young leaf and its axillary buds in the young stage is called _______.
questions.question_raw: The small lateral outgrowth of the leaf base which protects the young leaf and its axillary buds in the young stage is called _______.
questions.explanation: The leaf base bear two lateral small leaf like structures called stipules.

questions.question: Potato is a modified______.
questions.question_raw: Potato is a modified______.
questions.explanation: Potato is a modified stem as it contains nodes in the form of eyes on potato tubers which germinate to produce new potato plants if placed in suitable conditions.

questions.question: The type of aestivation shown by the diagram below is
questions.question_raw: The type of aestivation shown by the diagram below is
questions.explanation:
The diagram shows which is vexillary aestivation.
Vexillary aestivation is a mode of aestivation in which one large upper petal folds over, and covers, the other smaller petals, as in most papilionaceous plants.

questions.question: The axillary buds of stems may also get modified into woody, straight and pointed thorns in______.
questions.question_raw: The axillary buds of stems may also get modified into woody, straight and pointed thorns in______.
questions.explanation: The axillary buds of stems may also get modified into woody, straight, and pointed thorns in Citrus and Bougainvillea. They protect plants from browsing animals.

questions.question: A lateral branch with short internodes and each node bearing a rosette of leaves and a tuft of roots are found in _____.
questions.question_raw: A lateral branch with short internodes and each node bearing a rosette of leaves and a tuft of roots are found in _____.
questions.explanation: A lateral branch with short internodes and each node bearing a rosette of leaves and a tuft of roots are found in aquatic plants such as Pistia and Eicchornia.

questions.question: Which part of the plant help to hold the blade to light?
questions.question_raw: Which part of the plant help to hold the blade to light?
questions.explanation: The petiole help hold the blade to light. Long thin flexible petioles allow leaf blades to flutter in wind, thereby cooling the leaf and bringing fresh air to the leaf surface.

questions.question: Which part of the plant store food in garlic?
questions.question_raw: Which part of the plant store food in garlic?
questions.explanation: The fleshy leaves of onion and garlic store food.

questions.question:
The correct arrangement of the zones as seen in the vertical section of root is
 Root hair zone
 Zone of meristem
 Root cap zone
 Zone of maturation
 Zone of elongation
questions.question_raw:
The correct arrangement of the zones as seen in the vertical section of root is

  1. Root hair zone
  2. Zone of meristem
  3. Root cap zone
  4. Zone of maturation
  5. Zone of elongation
questions.explanation: The correct arrangement of the zones as seen in the vertical section of root is root cap zone, region of meristematic activity, region of elongation, region of maturation and root hair region.

questions.question: Phylloclade are:
questions.question_raw: Phylloclade are:

options.label: A
options.text: Green succulent stems of indefinite growth

correct_option_texts: Green succulent stems of indefinite growth
questions.explanation: Phylloclade are green succulent stems of indefinite growth that perform the function of photosynthesis due to the presence of chlorophyll.

questions.question: Select the insectivorous plant from the following:
questions.question_raw: Select the insectivorous plant from the following:
questions.explanation: Those plants that eat insect to fulfil its protein requirement are called insectivorous plants. They have modified leaves to capture insects. Drosera is an insectivorous plant.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Morphology of Flowering Plants

questions.question: 120^o phyllotaxy occurs in _________.
questions.question_raw: 120o phyllotaxy occurs in _________.
questions.explanation: Phyllotaxy is the arrangement of leaves on a plant stem. 120^o phyllotaxy occurs in a Tristichous arrangement in which three leaves are arranged at each node.

questions.question: Parallel venation is present in _________.
questions.question_raw: Parallel venation is present in _________.
questions.explanation: Parallel venation is the characteristic features of monocots. Dicots possess reticulate venation. The arrangement of veins on the leaf is called venation.

questions.question: Which is not a function of leaves?
questions.question_raw: Which is not a function of leaves?
questions.explanation: Leaves perform the function of photosynthesis, exchange of gases, and transpiration to lose excess of water. The formation of new buds is not a function of leaves.

questions.question: In Ocimum sanctum (tulsi), stem is_____.
questions.question_raw: In Ocimum sanctum (tulsi), stem is_____.
questions.explanation: The stem of Ocimum sanctum or tulsi is quadrangular in shape. The stem is covered with hairy projection.

questions.question: Bladders of Utricularia and Pitchers of Nepenthes are a modification of______.
questions.question_raw: Bladders of Utricularia and Pitchers of Nepenthes are a modification of______.
questions.explanation: Bladders of Utricularia and Pitcher of Nepenthes are a modification of leaves. They are modified to catch insects that are insectivorous plants.

questions.question: A plant in which adventitious roots develop chlorophyll and perform photosynthesis is______.
questions.question_raw: A plant in which adventitious roots develop chlorophyll and perform photosynthesis is______.
questions.explanation: Dischidia

questions.question: Inflorescence which shows indefinite growth and bears a number of flowers due to the active growing point is called ________.
questions.question_raw: Inflorescence which shows indefinite growth and bears a number of flowers due to the active growing point is called ________.
questions.explanation: In racemose inflorescence indefinite growth occurs and bears a number of flowers due to the active growing point.

questions.question: A scar on the seed coat through which the developing seeds were attached to the fruit is called _________.
questions.question_raw: A scar on the seed coat through which the developing seeds were attached to the fruit is called _________.
questions.explanation: The hilum is a scar on the seed coat through which the developing seeds were attached to the fruit.

questions.question: Name the type of thalamus in which the ovary is at top and stamens, petals and sepals are born below.
questions.question_raw: Name the type of thalamus in which the ovary is at top and stamens, petals and sepals are born below.
questions.explanation: In hypergamous flowers, the ovary is at the tops and stamens, petals, and sepals are borne below on the thalamus. The ovary is called superior.

questions.question: The fruit develops to form a whole inflorescence is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The fruit develops to form a whole inflorescence is called ________.
questions.explanation: True fruit is the fruit that develops from ovary only. In composite fruits, the whole inflorescence develops into fruits as in case of jackfruit.

questions.question: The cup-like structure formed by the adnation of sepals, petals, and stamens is ______.
questions.question_raw: The cup-like structure formed by the adnation of sepals, petals, and stamens is ______.
questions.explanation: In angiosperms, a hypanthium is a structure where basal portions of the calyx, the corolla, and the stamens form a cup-shaped tube.

questions.question: Ancestors of mammals belong to________.
questions.question_raw: Ancestors of mammals belong to________.
questions.explanation: The ancestor of mammals belongs to class Therapsida. Therapsida is a group of synapsids that includes mammals and their ancestors.

questions.question: Bat is member of an order________.
questions.question_raw: Bat is member of an order________.
questions.explanation: Bat is a member of the order Chiroptera of class Mammalia. Bats are only mammals to have achieved powered flight in which forelimb is modified as wings.

questions.question: Which one of the following has the highest number of species in nature?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following has the highest number of species in nature?
questions.explanation: Insects have the highest number of species in nature. Insect belongs to arthropods family of class Mammalia. They are characterized by jointed legs.

questions.question: One animal that does not perform locomotion is ______.
questions.question_raw: One animal that does not perform locomotion is ______.
questions.explanation: Sycon does not perform locomotion, although it is an animal. Sycon remains attached to the substratum and does not move from one place to another.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Morphology of Flowering Plants

questions.question: The locomotory organ of Annelida is__________.
questions.question_raw: The locomotory organ of Annelida is__________.
questions.explanation: The locomotory organ of Annelida is parapodia. Parapodium is in the form of lateral fleshy protrusions on the body surface that help in movement and capturing food.

questions.question: The larvae of urochordates are called:
questions.question_raw: The larvae of urochordates are called:
questions.explanation: The larvae of urochordates are called tadpole. Tadpole change into adults by the process of metamorphosis. The structure of tadpole is totally different from the adult ones.

questions.question: Silk produced by Antheraea mylitta is called:
questions.question_raw: Silk produced by Antheraea mylitta is called:
questions.explanation: The silk produced by Antheraeamylitta is called Tasar silk. This silk is considered as the most durable and best quality silk.

questions.question: The limbless amphibian is______.
questions.question_raw: The limbless amphibian is______.
questions.explanation: Ichthyophis is an amphibian which is limbless. Sometimes called as Asian caecilians. They are found in southeast Asia and the southern Philippines.

questions.question: In an inflorescence where flowers are borne laterally in an acropetal succession, the position of the youngest floral bud in the floral axis shall be
questions.question_raw: In an inflorescence where flowers are borne laterally in an acropetal succession, the position of the youngest floral bud in the floral axis shall be
questions.explanation: In the acropetal succession of an inflorescence, the youngest floral buds are found at the distal end and the oldest flowers are found at the proximal end.

questions.question: In which type of placentation, the ovary is unilocular with a single ovule?
questions.question_raw: In which type of placentation, the ovary is unilocular with a single ovule?
questions.explanation: In basal placentation, the placenta develops at the base of the ovary and a single ovule is attached to it.

questions.question: The Aleurone layer of Maize grain is especially rich in_________.
questions.question_raw: The Aleurone layer of Maize grain is especially rich in_________.
questions.explanation: Aleurone layer of maize grain is rich in proteins. It is the outermost layer of the endosperm followed by the inner starchy endosperm.

questions.question: Individual component of the perianth are called:
questions.question_raw: Individual component of the perianth are called:
questions.explanation: The flowers in which sepals and petals are not differentiated are called perianth. The individual component of the perianth is called Tepals.

questions.question: The condensed part of the stem which is specialized to take part in sexual reproduction of angiosperm is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The condensed part of the stem which is specialized to take part in sexual reproduction of angiosperm is called ________.
questions.explanation: The condensed part of the stem which is specialized to take part in sexual reproduction of angiosperms is called flower. Flower produce seeds and fruits for the propagation of species.

questions.question: The wall of fruit is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The wall of fruit is called ________.
questions.explanation: Fruits are the mature ovary or ovaries of one or more flowers. In fleshy fruits, the outer layer (which is often edible) is the pericarp, which is the tissue that develops from the ovary wall of the flower and surrounds the seeds.

questions.question: The mature seeds of plants such as gram and peas, possess no endosperm, because
questions.question_raw: The mature seeds of plants such as gram and peas, possess no endosperm, because

options.label: A
options.text: Endosperm gets used up by the developing embryo during seed development.

options.label: C
options.text: Endosperm is not formed in them.

options.label: D
options.text: Double fertilization does not take place in the plants.

correct_option_texts: Endosperm gets used up by the developing embryo during seed development.
questions.explanation: The mature seeds of plants do not possess endosperm because, it gets used up by the developing embryo during seed development.

questions.question: The single cotyledon of cereal grain is_______.
questions.question_raw: The single cotyledon of cereal grain is_______.
questions.explanation: In monocotyledonous seeds, the single cotyledon is called as scutellum. It is found in cereal grains like rice, wheat, maize and other monocots.

questions.question: Identify the correct one with respect to the symmetry of the flower.
questions.question_raw: Identify the correct one with respect to the symmetry of the flower.
questions.explanation: When a flower is divided into two equal radial halves in any radial plane passing through the centre, it is said to be actinomorphic e.g., mustard, datura, chilli.

questions.question: The ovary belonging to a single free carpel is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The ovary belonging to a single free carpel is called ________.
questions.explanation: When more than one carpel is present, they may be free, it is called apocarpous.

questions.question: Parenchymatous cushion present inside the ovary, where ovules are borne is called:
questions.question_raw: Parenchymatous cushion present inside the ovary, where ovules are borne is called:
questions.explanation: The placenta is the parenchymatous cushion present inside the ovary, ovules are borne. Ovules are attached with an ovary at the placenta to get nourishment.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Morphology of Flowering Plants

questions.question: The parietal placentation is seen in:
questions.question_raw: The parietal placentation is seen in:
questions.explanation: In parietal placentation, the ovules develop on the inner wall of the ovary or on the peripheral part. The ovary is one-chambered but it becomes two-chambered due to the formation of the false septum, e.g., mustard and Argemone.

questions.question: The form of the thalamus in which the ovary is at top and stamens, petals, and sepals are borne below is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The form of the thalamus in which the ovary is at top and stamens, petals, and sepals are borne below is called ________.
questions.explanation: In the hypogynous flower, the gynoecium occupies the highest position while the other parts are situated below it. The ovary in such flowers is said to be superior.

questions.question: Which plant shows valvate aestivation?
questions.question_raw: Which plant shows valvate aestivation?
questions.explanation: When sepals or petals in a whorl just touch one another at the margin, without overlapping, as in Calotropis, it is said to be valvate.

questions.question: When the stamens in a flower remain free, it is said to be______.
questions.question_raw: When the stamens in a flower remain free, it is said to be______.
questions.explanation: The stamens in a flower may either remain free (polyandrous) or maybe united in varying degrees.

questions.question: Mango is an example of_______.
questions.question_raw: Mango is an example of_______.
questions.explanation: Drupe is an indehiscent fruit in which an outer fleshy part and surrounds a shell of hardened endocarp with a seed. Mango is an example of a drupe.

questions.question: Edible part of Coconut is:
questions.question_raw: Edible part of Coconut is:
questions.explanation: The edible part of Coconut is endosperm and embryo. The water inside the coconut is a cellular embryo and white kernel surrounding it is the main edible part that is the endosperm.

questions.question: A seed is made up of
questions.question_raw: A seed is made up of

options.label: D
options.text: A seed coat and an embryo

correct_option_texts: A seed coat and an embryo
questions.explanation: A seed is made up of a seed coat and an embryo.

questions.question: If the margin of thalamus grows upward enclosing the ovary completely and getting fused with it, the other parts of flower arise above the ovary, the flower is said to be:
questions.question_raw: If the margin of thalamus grows upward enclosing the ovary completely and getting fused with it, the other parts of flower arise above the ovary, the flower is said to be:
questions.explanation: In epigynous flowers, the margin of the thalamus grows upward enclosing the ovary completely and getting fused with it, the other parts of the flower arise above the ovary.

questions.question: The diagram below shows
questions.question_raw: The diagram below shows
questions.explanation: If gynoecium is situated in the centre and otherparts of the flower are located on the rim of the thalamus almost at thesame level, it is called perigynous. The ovary here is said to be half inferior.

questions.question: When the ovules develop on the inner wall of the ovary, the placentation is______.
questions.question_raw: When the ovules develop on the inner wall of the ovary, the placentation is______.
questions.explanation: In parietal placentation, the ovules develop on the inner wall of the ovary or on the peripheral part.

questions.question: The aestivation seen in Solanaceae is_____.
questions.question_raw: The aestivation seen in Solanaceae is_____.
questions.explanation: Valvate aestivation is seen in the family Solanaceae.

questions.question: Dry indehiscent single-seeded fruit formed by bicarpellarysyncarpous inferior ovary is in the diagram given below, the parts A and B are respectively,
questions.question_raw: Dry indehiscent single-seeded fruit formed by bicarpellarysyncarpous inferior ovary is in the diagram given below, the parts A and B are respectively,
questions.explanation: The part is identified as Hilum and Micropyle.

questions.question: Which one is a monocarpic in nature?
questions.question_raw: Which one is a monocarpic in nature?
questions.explanation: Monocarpic plants are those that flower, set seeds, and then die. Bamboo is monocarpic in nature that flowers once in a lifetime.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a true nut?
questions.question_raw: Which one of the following is a true nut?
questions.explanation:
A true nut is a fruit composed of a hard shell and an edible seed. The shell does not open to release the seed (indehiscent), e.g., hazelnut, chestnuts.
Walnuts, almonds and pecans are drupes. The fruit of the coconut is a large, dry drupe. Cashew nuts are not true nuts. In the cashew nut, the ovary develops outside of the fruit (false fruit). The groundnut or peanut is not a true nut, but rather a legume. In groundnut, edible the cotyledons remain below ground and act as a storage organ.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Morphology of Flowering Plants

questions.question: Largest bud is found in______.
questions.question_raw: Largest bud is found in______.
questions.explanation: Cabbage is the bud of the cabbage plant. Cabbage bud is considered as largest bud present in angiosperms plants. A successive layer of leaves covers the buds to form the round structure on condensed stem.

questions.question: The white, translucent, fleshy, and edible structure present between seed and pericarp is _____.
questions.question_raw: The white, translucent, fleshy, and edible structure present between seed and pericarp is _____.
questions.explanation: The white translucent, fleshy and edible structure present between seed and pericarp is called an aril. It is found in a mace of the nutmeg seed.

questions.question: Study of seed is called as______.
questions.question_raw: Study of seed is called as______.
questions.explanation: The study of seed is called Spermology. Study of seed includes shape, size, colour and viability.

questions.question: The edible part of the apple is:
questions.question_raw: The edible part of the apple is:
questions.explanation: The edible part of the apple is fleshy thalamus. Apple is an example of false fruit as it contains thalamus along with ovary.

questions.question: A chambered fruit formed from an inferior ovary and having succulent testa is_______.
questions.question_raw: A chambered fruit formed from an inferior ovary and having succulent testa is_______.
questions.explanation: Pomegranate is the fruit which is formed from inferior ovary having succulent testa. Chambered compartment bears many seeds generally red in colour when ripened.

questions.question: When the several pistils of the same flower are separate and the ovary belongs to a single free carpel, it is called _________.
questions.question_raw: When the several pistils of the same flower are separate and the ovary belongs to a single free carpel, it is called _________.
questions.explanation: When the several pistils of the same flower are separate and the ovary belongs to a single free carpel, it is called apocarpous.

questions.question: When the several pistils of the same flower are separate and the ovary belongs to a single free carpel, it is called _________.
questions.question_raw: When the several pistils of the same flower are separate and the ovary belongs to a single free carpel, it is called _________.
questions.explanation: When the several pistils of the same flower are separate and the ovary belongs to a single free carpel, it is called apocarpous.

questions.question: Sweet potato and potato are an example of:
questions.question_raw: Sweet potato and potato are an example of:
questions.explanation: Sweet potato and potato are an example of analogous organs as they perform the same function that is storing food. But potato is a modified stem but sweet potato is modified root.

questions.question: Many pulses of daily use belong to one of the families below (tick the correct answer)
questions.question_raw: Many pulses of daily use belong to one of the families below (tick the correct answer)
questions.explanation: Fabaceae are the sources of pulses such as gram, arhar, sem, moong, soybean.

questions.question: The floral formula P_3+3 A_3+3 G_[3] belongs to
questions.question_raw: The floral formula   P3+3 A3+3 G[3]  belongs to
questions.explanation: In Liliaceae, the flower is bisexual and actinomorphic. There are 6 (3+3) tepals and 6 (3+3) stamens with a superior ovary and trilocular with many ovules.

questions.question: The dye plant belonging to family Fabaceae is______.
questions.question_raw: The dye plant belonging to family Fabaceae is______.
questions.explanation: Indigofera is a dye belonging to family Fabaceae.

questions.question: Pentamerous, actinomorphic flower, bicarpellary ovary with oblique septum and berry or capsule fruit belong to the family______.
questions.question_raw: Pentamerous, actinomorphic flower, bicarpellary ovary with oblique septum and berry or capsule fruit belong to the family______.
questions.explanation: The members of family Solanaceae bears flower which is pentamerous, actinomorphic, bicarpellary, ovary with oblique septum and berry, or capsule fruit. It includes potato, brinjal, etc.

questions.question: Hypanthodium is found in the genus is ________.
questions.question_raw: Hypanthodium is found in the genus is ________.
questions.explanation: Hypanthodium is a special type of inflorescence in which receptacle is fleshy and forms a hollow ball-like structure with an apical opening. This kind of inflorescence is found in genus ficus.

questions.question: A plant having male, female and inter-sexual flowers is called ________.
questions.question_raw: A plant having male, female and inter-sexual flowers is called ________.
questions.explanation: A plant having male, female and inter-sexual flowers is called polygamous flower. The same plant bears all three kinds of flowers.

questions.question: A flower which can be divided into two equal vertical halves by only one plane is called ________.
questions.question_raw: A flower which can be divided into two equal vertical halves by only one plane is called ________.
questions.explanation: The symmetry in which a flower can be divided into two equal vertical halves by only one plane is called zygomorphic flower as in pea.

tests.category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Biology > Morphology of Flowering Plants

questions.question: What is samara?
questions.question_raw: What is samara?

options.label: A
options.text: Fruits having wings formed from other structure

correct_option_texts: Fruits having wings formed from other structure
questions.explanation: Samara is a winged achene, a type of fruit which a flattened wing of fibrous, papery tissue develops from the ovary wall.

questions.question: Unipinnate compound leaves are present in:
questions.question_raw: Unipinnate compound leaves are present in:
questions.explanation: A pinnately compound leaves are one which the midrib bears laterally a number of leaflets. Unipinnate compound leaves are present in neem plant.

questions.question: Placentation in a syncarpous, unilocular ovary bearing two or more placentae longitudinally along the wall is called ________.
questions.question_raw: Placentation in a syncarpous, unilocular ovary bearing two or more placentae longitudinally along the wall is called ________.
questions.explanation: Placentation in a syncarpous, unilocular ovary bearing two or more placentae longitudinally along the wall is called parietal placentation.

questions.question: Dry indehiscent single-seeded fruit formed by the bi-carpellary syncarpous inferior ovary is______.
questions.question_raw: Dry indehiscent single-seeded fruit formed by the bi-carpellary syncarpous inferior ovary is______.
questions.explanation: Cypsela is a single-seeded dry indehiscent fruit that develops one form part of the inferior ovary which is bicarpellary and syncarpous.

questions.question: The science that deals with the study of form, size, colour, structure, and relative position of various parts of an organism is called _________.
questions.question_raw: The science that deals with the study of form, size, colour, structure, and relative position of various parts of an organism is called _________.
questions.explanation: Morphology is the branch of science in which we study about form, size, colour, structure and relative position of various parts of organisms.

questions.question: Name the smallest angiosperm.
questions.question_raw: Name the smallest angiosperm.
questions.explanation: The smallest angiosperm plant is Wolffia. It is an aquatic plant, free-floating, green or yellow-green and without roots.

questions.question: The small lateral outgrowth of the leaf base which protects the young leaf and its axillary buds in the young stage is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The small lateral outgrowth of the leaf base which protects the young leaf and its axillary buds in the young stage is called ________.
questions.explanation: Stipules are the small lateral outgrowth of the leaf base which protect the young leaf and its axillary buds in the young stage. It is a green leafy structure.

questions.question: Which one is a marine angiosperm?
questions.question_raw: Which one is a marine angiosperm?
questions.explanation: Zostera is a marine angiosperms plant. The plants have broad leaves that help them in floating on the water surface, the root is poorly developed.

questions.question: Among pea tendril, lemon thorn, and Opuntia spines, which one are homologous structures?
questions.question_raw: Among pea tendril, lemon thorn, and Opuntia spines, which one are homologous structures?
questions.explanation: Homologous structures are those structures in which they have common origin and structure but perform different functions. Pea tendrils and Opuntia spines are homologous structures.

questions.question: The main plant body of banana is:
questions.question_raw: The main plant body of banana is:
questions.explanation: The main plant body of banana is an aggregation of leaf base. The stem is highly reduced and rudimentary present at the base of the plant body.

questions.question: Phylloclade are:
questions.question_raw: Phylloclade are:

options.label: A
options.text: Green succulent stems of indefinite growth

correct_option_texts: Green succulent stems of indefinite growth
questions.explanation: Phylloclade are green succulent stems of indefinite growth that perform the function of photosynthesis due to the presence of chlorophyll.

questions.question: Which is not a function of leaves?
questions.question_raw: Which is not a function of leaves?
questions.explanation: Leaves perform the function of photosynthesis, exchange of gases, and transpiration to lose excess of water. The formation of new buds is not a function of leaves.

questions.question: Silk produced by Antheraea mylitta is called:
questions.question_raw: Silk produced by Antheraea mylitta is called:
questions.explanation: The silk produced by Antheraeamylitta is called Tasar silk. This silk is considered as the most durable and best quality silk.

questions.question: Which plant shows valvate aestivation?
questions.question_raw: Which plant shows valvate aestivation?
questions.explanation: When sepals or petals in a whorl just touch one another at the margin, without overlapping, as in Calotropis, it is said to be valvate.

questions.question: A plant having male, female and inter-sexual flowers is called ________.
questions.question_raw: A plant having male, female and inter-sexual flowers is called ________.
questions.explanation: A plant having male, female and inter-sexual flowers is called polygamous flower. The same plant bears all three kinds of flowers.
